title: Minimize the admission of containers which use HostPorts

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers which use HostPorts
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers which require the use of HostPorts.
input_row.rationale: Host ports connect containers directly to the host's network. This can bypass controls such as network policy. There should be at least one admission control policy defined which does not permit containers which require the use of HostPorts. If you need to run containers which require HostPorts, this should be defined in a separate policy and you should carefully check to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to use that policy. Impact: Pods defined with hostPort settings in either the container, initContainer or ephemeralContainer sections will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.
input_row.audit: List the policies in use for each namespace in the cluster, ensure that each policy disallows the admission of containers which have hostPort sections.
input_row.remediation: Add policies to each namespace in the cluster which has user workloads to restrict the admission of containers which use hostPort sections. Default Value: By default, there are no restrictions on the use of HostPorts. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/security/pod-security-standards/ 5.3 Network Policies and CNI
input_row.impact: Pods defined with hostPort settings in either the container, initContainer or ephemeralContainer sections will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific policy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_host_ports_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **Objective:** Ensure that each namespace with user workloads has at least one Pod Security Admission (PSA) policy or equivalent admission control policy that restricts the admission of containers using hostPort in any container type (containers, initContainers, ephemeralContainers).
- **Kubernetes API calls / kubectl commands:**
  - Enumerate all namespaces: `kubectl get namespaces`
  - For each namespace, check for Pod Security Admission labels:
    - `kubectl get namespace <ns> -o jsonpath='{.metadata.labels}'`
    - Look for `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce` or `pod-security.kubernetes.io/audit`
    - Confirm the value is `restricted` or a custom policy that denies hostPort usage.
  - If using Gatekeeper/OPA or Kyverno, enumerate ConstraintTemplates and Constraints:
    - `kubectl get constrainttemplates`
    - `kubectl get <constraint-kind> -A`
    - Parse constraint specs for hostPort restrictions.
  - Optionally, enumerate PodSecurityPolicies (if legacy PSP is enabled):
    - `kubectl get psp -o yaml`
    - Check `.spec.hostPorts` is empty or not set (denies all hostPorts).
- **Validation:**
  - For each namespace, confirm that the enforced Pod Security Admission level is `restricted` (which blocks hostPort usage), or that a custom admission policy (OPA/Kyverno/PSP) is present and configured to deny hostPort usage in all container types.
  - If any namespace lacks such a policy, or the policy allows hostPort usage, mark as non-compliant.
- **Pass Criteria:** Every namespace with user workloads has an enforced policy (PSA, OPA, Kyverno, or PSP) that denies the admission of pods with hostPort set in any container, initContainer, or ephemeralContainer.
- **Fail Criteria:** Any namespace with user workloads does not have such a policy, or the policy allows hostPort usage.
- **Example CLI:**
  - `kubectl get ns --show-labels | grep pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted`
  - For OPA/Kyverno: Parse constraint/policy definitions for hostPort deny rules.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11 PDF
